normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem dist_length (dist : Vector (Option Nat) n) : dist.length = n := by simpa using dist.length
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length n.
Distances are defined for every vertex index v in Fin n.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n) (k : Nat), dist[v] = some k → (∃ (path : List (Fin n)), path.head? = some source ∧ path.Last? = some v ∧ path.length - 1 = k ∧ ∀ i : Nat, i + 1 < path.length → (adj path.get ⟨i, by ...⟩).contains (path.get ⟨i+1, by ...⟩))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some k, then there exists a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n), (∃ (k : Nat), ∃ (path : List (Fin n)), path.head? = some source ∧ path.Last? = some v ∧ path.length - 1 = k ∧ ∀ i : Nat, i + 1 < path.length → (adj path.get ⟨i, by ...⟩).contains (path.get ⟨i+1, by ...⟩)) → ∃ (k : Nat), dist[v] = some k
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If there exists any directed path from source to v, then dist[v] is Some k for some k.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n), (¬∃ (k : Nat), ∃ (path : List (Fin n)), path.head? = some source ∧ path.Last? = some v ∧ path.length - 1 = k ∧ ∀ i : Nat, i + 1 < path.length → (adj path.get ⟨i, by ...⟩).contains (path.get ⟨i+1, by ...⟩)) → dist[v] = none
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If v is unreachable from source, then dist[v] = None.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n) (k : Nat), dist[v] = some k → (∀ (k' : Nat), (∃ (path : List (Fin n)), path.head? = some source ∧ path.Last? = some v ∧ path.length - 1 = k' ∧ ∀ i : Nat, i + 1 < path.length → (adj path.get ⟨i, by ...⟩).contains (path.get ⟨i+1, by ...⟩)) → k ≤ k')
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some k, then k is the minimum number of edges among all paths from source to v.
The distance from the source to itself is Some 0.
